revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:稲毛浅間神社]] {{Infobox religious building| |Name=Asama Shrine |image=File:Inagesengenshrine1.jpg |Location={{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}} {{ill|Inage-ku|en|Inage-ku|qid=Q1075905|lt=Inage Ward|1=Inage-ku}} Inage 1-15-10 |ISO=JP-12 |Latitude degrees=35|Latitude minutes=38|Latitude seconds=11.39 |Longitude degrees=140|Longitude minutes=4|Longitude seconds=57.14 |Enshrined deities= * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
* {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}
* {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} ({{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|de|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|サルタビコノカミ|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|lt_ja=サルタビコノカミ|lt_de=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village Shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Main hall= |Alternate name=Inage Sengen Shrine, Inage Sengen Shrine |Inscriptive sites= |Established={{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 3 ({{ill|AD 808|en|AD 808|qid=Q30366|lt=808|1=AD 808}}) |Annual festival={{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} |Rituals= |Label position=bottom |Map=Japan Chiba |prefecture={{ill|Inage-ku|qid=Q1075905|1=Inage-ku}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.6365|N|140.08252777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} }} {{Coordinates List}} [[File:Inagesengenshrine2.jpg|thumb|Second torii]] '''Inage Sengen Shrine''' (稻毛浅間神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Inage, within {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Inage area. Its former shrine Rank was that of a village shrine, and on registration the name of the religious corporation is '''Asama Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ). It has been selected as one of the {{ill|Chiba 100 Heritage|de|Chiba 100 Heritage|ja|ちば遺産100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba 100 Heritage|1=100 Treasures of Chiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11271817}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deity ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * Subordinate Deities ** {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} ({{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|de|Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja|サルタビコノカミ|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|lt_ja=サルタビコノカミ|lt_de=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == History == It is said to have been founded in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} era ({{ill|AD 808|en|AD 808|qid=Q30366|lt=808|1=AD 808}}), when the enshrinement was initiated from {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|en|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|qid=Q653180|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}. In the 4th year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|qid=Q1194671|lt=Jishō|1=Jishō}} era ({{ill|1180|en|1180|qid=Q19702|lt=1180|1=1180}}), when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} dispatched his envoy, Higashi Rokurō Tamenori, to donate sacred regalia and pray for enduring martial success, the steadfast faith of the Chiba clan—since the time of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|de|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|lt=Chiba Tsunetane|lt_ja=千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406057}}—is attested by ancient records. During the 3rd year of the {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|qid=Q1194415|lt=Bunchi|1=Bunji (era)}} era ({{ill|1187|en|1187|qid=Q19731|lt=1187|1=1187}}), when the shrine building was reconstructed, the grounds were earthenly mounded to resemble the shape of Mount {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}}, and the approach was laid out in imitation of a Mount Fuji climbing trail, with the building erected facing Mount Fuji across {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|qid=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay|1=Tokyo Bay}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the precincts extended 400 ken (approximately 720 meters) east–west, but with the advent of the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, only the current shrine grounds (6,400 tsubo) were retained and elevated. In September {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Showa 39), an unexplained fire destroyed the shrine building reconstructed in the Bunchi era; it was rebuilt in {{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41), which is the present structure. In {{ill|1986|en|1986|qid=Q2430|lt=1986|1=1986}} (Showa 61), on the occasion of the 60th year of Emperor Showa’s reign, expansion and retaining wall work was carried out in the front courtyard, and in October {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} (Heisei 26) a shrine gate was erected to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the reconstruction of Inage Sengen Shrine. == Primary Torii == [[File:TORI1.JPG|thumb|Torii located on the opposite side of the {{ill|Chiba Kaidō|de|Chiba-Straße|ja|千葉街道|lt=Chiba Kaidō|lt_ja=千葉街道|lt_de=Chiba Road|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Kaidō|1=Chiba Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406755}} from the second torii]] Although the official website does not mention it, it is said that a torii once existed over the sea. Currently, on the opposite side of the {{ill|Chiba Kaidō|de|Chiba-Straße|ja|千葉街道|lt=Chiba Kaidō|lt_ja=千葉街道|lt_de=Chiba Road|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Kaidō|1=Chiba Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406755}} from the second torii, a torii donated by the president of {{ill|Manyo-ken|de|Manyo-ken|ja|万葉軒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manyo-ken|1=Manyo-ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353918}} stands.On seaside revitalization ({{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}) https://www.city.chiba.jp/somu/shichokoshitsu/hisho/documents/161018seikeikonwakai.pdf == Kagura of Asama Shrine == The {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} of Asama Shrine was designated in 1962 (Showa 37) as a {{ill|prefectural designated intangible folk cultural property|de|vom Präfektur festgelegtes immaterielles Volkskulturdenkmal|ja|県指定無形民俗文化財|lt=Prefectural Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=県指定無形民俗文化財|lt_de=Prefectural Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural designated intangible folk cultural property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by Chiba Prefecture. The Twelve-Group Kagura handed down at Inage Sengen Shrine is said to have been taught in {{ill|1504|en|1504|qid=Q6148|lt=1504|1=1504}} (Eishō 1) by a renowned kagura master from Kyushu to the then-priest, Ōkoshi Kuranosuke, and thereafter transmitted among the villagers, evolving into a form unique to this shrine. This kagura comprises twelve groups, beginning with a dance (maiden’s dance) to welcome the deity and ending with a sending-off dance accompanied by festival music. Based on Japanese mythology, its performance—employing masks (except in the maiden’s dance) and proceeding in a silent, theatrical style—is said to have been influenced by the refined Edo kagura.{{Cite web|language=ja|title=Kagura of Asama Shrine|url=http://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/sengenjinjya-kagura.html|website=Chiba City|accessdate=2019-02-20|last=Chiba City}} == Inage Pine Grove == [[File:Inage-sengenjinja-sando.jpg|thumb|Approach surrounded by a pine grove]] This pine grove, which has gradually formed since the establishment of Asama Shrine, was once a site with breathtaking views where the waves lapped very close to shore. The hilltop area encompassing the shrine precincts has become a noted scenic spot, with part of it open to the public as a city park.{{Cite web|language=ja|title=Inage Pine Grove (City-designated Scenic Spot)|url=http://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/inagematsubayashi.html|website=Chiba City|accessdate=2019-02-20|last=Chiba City}} In {{ill|1960|en|1960|qid=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Showa 35), it was designated as a city-designated natural monument as well as a commemorative and scenic site. == Shrine Precincts == === Sub‐shrines within the Precincts === ; Minor Shrines * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) * {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|de|Ōmiya-Schrein|ja|大宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} ({{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Kogodake Shrine ({{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} ({{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|de|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|lt_de=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}) ; Combined Halls ** {{ill|Fujimuro Asama Shrine|de|Fujimuro Asama-Schrein|ja|冨士御室浅間神社|lt=Komuro Shrine|lt_ja=小室神社|lt_de=Komuro Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimuro Asama Shrine|1=Fuji Omuro Asama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395081}} ({{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|de|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|lt_ja=木花咲耶姫命|lt_de=Konohananosakuyahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1781862}}) ** {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|de|Wakamiya-Schrein|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|qid=Q1051960|lt=Horihotodemikoto|1=Hoori}}) ** {{ill|Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Jingū|lt=Katori Shrine|de|Katori-Schrein|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}) ; Others * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|de|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1781862}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}) is enshrined before the primary torii. * {{ill|Shinmeisha|de|Shinmeisha|ja|神明社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590310}} ({{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu|1=Amaterasu}}) * {{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|de|Mitsumine-Schrein|ja|三峯神社|lt=, see below|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}) * {{ill|Loyalty Monument|de|Loyalty Monument|ja|忠魂碑|lt=Loyalty Monument|lt_ja=忠霊碑|lt_de=Loyalty Monument|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Loyalty Monument|1=monument to the loyal dead|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11274518}} * {{ill|Water Shrine|de|Water Shrine|ja|水神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Shrine|1=Suijingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549047}} * {{ill|Sanno Shrine|de|Sanno-Schrein|ja|山王神社|lt=Sanno Palace|lt_ja=山王宮|lt_de=Sanno Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanno Shrine|1=Sannō Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11470057}} * {{ill|Tenno Shrine|de|Tennō-Schrein|ja|天王神社|lt=Tennō Palace|lt_ja=天王宮|lt_de=Tennō Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine|qq=}} * Kofune Shrine ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}) * {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|qid=Q3082157|lt=Koshin-sama|1=Kōshin}} ({{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}) * {{ill|Shokugyōshinroku Stone Monument|de|Shokugyōshinroku Stone Monument|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokugyōshinroku Stone Monument|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}} Stone Monument * {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|qid=Q193461|lt=Miroku Bodhisattva|1=Maitreya}} === Gallery === File:Inagesengenshrine3.jpg|{{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} File:Inage-sengenjinja-chozuya.jpg|Chōzuya File:Inage-sengenjinja-hellokitty.JPG|Hello Kitty amulet == Transportation == === Public Transportation === ; Railway * {{ill|Keisei Electric Railway|en|Keisei Electric Railway|qid=Q83538|lt=Keisei Electric Railway|1=Keisei Electric Railway}}: {{ill|Keisei Inage Station|en|Keisei Inage Station|qid=Q11374398|lt=Keisei Inage Station|1=Keisei Inage Station}} ({{ill|Keisei Chiba Line|de|Keisei Chiba-Linie|ja|千葉線|lt=Keisei Chiba Line|lt_ja=京成千葉線|lt_de=Keisei Chiba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keisei Chiba Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京成千葉線|1=Keisei Chiba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q841998}}), on foot * {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}: {{ill|Inage Station|en|Inage Station|qid=Q909403|lt=Inage Station|1=Inage Station}}, on foot * JR East: {{ill|Inagekaigan Station|en|Inagekaigan Station|qid=Q2583249|lt=Inage Kaigan Station|1=Inagekaigan Station}} ({{ill|Keiyō Line|en|Keiyō Line|qid=Q741145|lt=Keiyo Line|1=Keiyō Line}}), on foot ; Bus * {{ill|Chiba City Bus|de|Chiba-Stadtbus|ja|ちばシティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京成バス千葉イースト新港営業所|1=Keisei Bus Chiba East Shinminato Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11271810}}: Proud City Line ** Ina50: {{ill|Inage Station|en|Inage Station|qid=Q909403|lt=Inage Station|1=Inage Station}} → '''Asama Shrine''' → Takasu Pool Front → Proud City – Yaoko Front → {{ill|Chiba West Police Station|de|Polizeistation Chiba West|ja|千葉西警察署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba West Police Station|1=Chiba-nishi Police Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406759}} → Masago Central Park → {{ill|Kemigawahama Station|en|Kemigawahama Station|qid=Q2493887|lt=Kemigawahama Station|1=Kemigawahama Station}} ** Ina51: {{ill|Kemigawahama Station|en|Kemigawahama Station|qid=Q2493887|lt=Kemigawahama Station|1=Kemigawahama Station}} → Masago Central Park → {{ill|Chiba West Police Station|de|Polizeistation Chiba West|ja|千葉西警察署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba West Police Station|1=Chiba-nishi Police Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406759}} → Proud City – West → Takasu Pool Front → '''Asama Shrine''' → {{ill|Inage Station|en|Inage Station|qid=Q909403|lt=Inage Station|1=Inage Station}} ** Ina52: {{ill|Inagekaigan Station|en|Inagekaigan Station|qid=Q2583249|lt=Inage Kaigan Station|1=Inagekaigan Station}} → Proud City – Yaoko Front → {{ill|Tokyo Dental College|en|Tokyo Dental College|qid=Q7813868|lt=Dental College|1=Tokyo Dental College}} Entrance → Takasu Pool Front → '''Asama Shrine''' → {{ill|Inage Station|en|Inage Station|qid=Q909403|lt=Inage Station|1=Inage Station}} ** Ina53: {{ill|Inage Station|en|Inage Station|qid=Q909403|lt=Inage Station|1=Inage Station}} → '''Asama Shrine''' → Takasu Pool Front → Proud City – Yaoko Front → Dental College Entrance → {{ill|Inagekaigan Station|en|Inagekaigan Station|qid=Q2583249|lt=Inage Kaigan Station|1=Inagekaigan Station}} ** Ina55: {{ill|Inage Station|en|Inage Station|qid=Q909403|lt=Inage Station|1=Inage Station}} → '''Asama Shrine''' → Takasu Pool Front → Proud City – Yaoko Front → Dental College Entrance === By Car === ; Highways * {{ill|Komatsugawa Route|en|Komatsugawa Route|qid=Q11667100|lt=Shuto Expressway Route 7 Komatsugawa Line|1=Komatsugawa Route}} ・ {{ill|Keiyō Road|en|Keiyō Road|qid=Q6385301|lt=Keiyo Road|1=Keiyō Road}}: {{ill|Makuhari Interchange|de|Makuhari-Ausfahrt|ja|幕張インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makuhari Interchange|1=Makuhari Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11060550}} * {{ill|Bayshore Route|en|Bayshore Route|qid=Q4874809|lt=Shuto Expressway Bayshore Route|1=Bayshore Route}} ・ {{ill|Higashi-Kantō Expressway|en|Higashi-Kantō Expressway|qid=Q864875|lt=Higashi-Kantō Expressway|1=Higashi-Kantō Expressway}}: {{ill|Bayshore Narashino Interchange|de|Bayshore Narashino-Ausfahrt|ja|湾岸習志野IC|lt=Bayshore Narashino Interchange|lt_ja=湾岸習志野インターチェンジ|lt_de=Bayshore Narashino Interchange|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bayshore Narashino Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湾岸習志野インターチェンジ|1=Wangan-Narashino interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2258189}} ; Local Roads * {{ill|Japan National Route 14|en|Japan National Route 14|qid=Q1056176|lt=Chiba Kaidō|1=Japan National Route 14}} * {{ill|Japan National Route 357|en|Japan National Route 357|qid=Q6157329|lt=Bayshore Road|1=Japan National Route 357}} * {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Route 134 Inage Station Inage Kaigan Line|de|Präfekturstraße 134 Inage|ja|千葉県道134号稲毛停車場稲毛海岸線|lt=Inage Station Inage Kaigan Line|lt_ja=稲毛停車場稲毛海岸線|lt_de=Inage Station Inage Kaigan Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Route 134 Inage Station Inage Kaigan Line|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 134|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406437}} ; Parking * Proceed 150 meters toward Chiba from the “Inage Sengen Shrine Front” intersection to find a free {{ill|Parking lot|en|Parking lot|qid=Q6501349|lt=Parking Lot|1=Parking lot}} (approximately 80 spaces available). == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture|de|Liste der Schreine in Chiba-Präfektur|ja|千葉県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406137}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * {{ill|Sengen
